commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual review is the right lane because this owner-authored low-level POSIX process cleanup has no narrow automated repair remaining and should wait for exact-head CI plus maintainer judgment.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain issue was found; the diff changes local process lifecycle code and tests without new dependencies, secrets handling, or CI permissions. (sha=7c7295ea8762e4829774a8793bed422eb220540d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces Kiro's `Process` runner with a package-internal `posix_spawn` process-group launcher, identity-gated residual-helper cleanup, EOF-aware pipe capture, and focused Kiro/process regressions.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. The linked reports and current-main baseline show the Kiro runner needed bounded process and pipe cleanup, and the PR adds fake-process regressions for the residual helper cases; I did not run live Kiro because AGENTS.md discourages credentialed provider probes.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is the right lane because this owner-authored low-level POSIX process cleanup has no narrow automated repair remaining and should wait for exact-head CI plus maintainer judgment.

target.title: fix: clean up Kiro CLI helper processes
target.body:
## Summary

- launch Kiro CLI commands with a dedicated process group established before `exec`
- terminate residual helpers after normal parent exit, escalating from `TERM` to `KILL` before draining inherited pipes
- discover escaped helpers by the exact stdout/stderr pipes they retain
- snapshot descendants and same-group members while the root process identity is still valid
- capture normal-exit process-group members before reaping the root
- signal only processes whose start-time identity still matches, avoiding PID or process-group reuse hazards
- preserve child standard streams when the host starts with a standard descriptor closed
- preserve existing timeout, cancellation, output, and exit-status behavior

Follow-up to https://github.com/steipete/CodexBar/issues/1533 and https://github.com/steipete/CodexBar/issues/1535.

## Root Cause

The original Kiro runner could wait indefinitely after termination or parent exit: `waitUntilExit()` had no `KILL` escalation, and final EOF reads remained blocked while descendants retained inherited output pipes.

The first bounded repair established a process group before `exec`, but helpers could still escape it with `setsid`, or become reparented while remaining in the original group. The final candidate records stable identities for output pipes, descendants, the root, and current process-group members while the root identity is known to be current. For normal exit, it observes the child with `waitid(WEXITED | WNOWAIT)`, captures same-group identities while the unreaped root still prevents PID/PGID reuse, and only then reaps the root. All later signals are sent only to those start-time-verified identities. No numeric process-group signal is sent after root exit.

## Proof

Exact head: `7c7295ea8762e4829774a8793bed422eb220540d`

- focused Kiro/process tests: 40 passed, including normal-exit same-group non-pipe helpers, inherited-pipe, escaped-session, reparented same-group, grace-period, ignored-`TERM`, cancellation, and closed-standard-descriptor cases
- `make test`: 470 deterministic selections across 40 shards passed; no Swift Testing issue markers
- `make check`: parser/package/release guards passed; SwiftFormat clean; SwiftLint 0 violations across 1117 files
- `git diff --check`: passed
- `swift build -c release --product CodexBarCLI --skip-update`: passed
- release CLI normal same-group proof: parent exited normally while a same-group helper closed both output descriptors and ignored `TERM`; the command returned parsed usage in 1.628s and removed the helper
- release CLI normal escaped-pipe proof: parent exited normally while a session-escaped helper retained inherited output pipes and ignored `HUP`/`TERM`; the command returned parsed usage in 2.002s and removed the helper
- release CLI idle-termination proof: a reparented same-group helper held no output pipes and ignored `TERM`; the command returned parsed usage in 11.026s and removed the helper
- all three live proofs returned exit 0, emitted no stderr, and produced parsed Kiro usage without an error
- release CLI: arm64 Mach-O, ad-hoc signed, SHA-256 `df2c82c7b235300d0cd0ad6a90de838711e91a8d2ada6201b7c1293dc11018b4`
- exact-diff local autoreview: no accepted or actionable findings
- Public Model Identifier Gate: PASS; the exact diff, added lines, build log, and public proof add no model identifiers, while broad matches in full logs and the binary are unchanged repository/provider surface

The runtime proof used an isolated temporary home and synthetic account data. It did not access live credentials, browser data, Keychain, private payloads, or user-identifying paths.

## Risk

The new process launcher is package-internal and currently used only by Kiro. Pipe-holder discovery runs only for residual groups or failed EOF drain. Darwin uses `proc_listpids` for process-group enumeration; Linux filters `/proc`. The pre-reap snapshot uses standardized `waitid`/`WNOWAIT`; exact-head CI validates both Linux architectures. Every terminating signal is gated by a stable process start identity, and normal successful exit cannot target unrelated members of a reused numeric process group.
